authoritative_sources.title: National Operations & Maintenance Scope of Work
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA is an authoritative federal facilities operator. Its national O&M specification defines CMMS training, work-order tracking, technician qualifications, shutdown notifications, impairment handling, and maintenance planning for facilities operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accepted work-order normalization and active-work controls

authoritative_sources.title: Operations & Maintenance Best Practices Guide: Release 3.0
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Energy Federal Energy Management Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOE FEMP is an authoritative federal facilities operations source. Its O&M guide defines CMMS capabilities, work-order generation and prioritization, scheduled versus unscheduled maintenance tracking, warranty information retention, and maintenance metrics relevant to facilities work-order execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Work-order tracking and maintenance evidence discipline
used_for: evidence needed for work-order progress and closure

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.20 - General safety and health provisions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. federal workplace safety regulator. This regulation requires accident-prevention programs and frequent inspections of job sites, materials, and equipment, which must constrain facilities execution-state decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Safety prerequisite and hold or reversal handling
used_for: separating work-order state management from safety authority

authoritative_sources.title: Maintaining Your Property
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: A local building department is an authority having jurisdiction. This NYC DOB guidance demonstrates that plumbing and similar code-sensitive work require licensed professionals and proper permits, establishing a clear boundary for facilities work-order execution.
used_for: refusal of unsupported code or permit claims

authoritative_sources.title: Mechanical Inspections
authoritative_sources.publisher: Seattle Department of Construction and Inspections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official SDCI inspection guidance provides concrete public examples of city-permitted HVAC inspection timing, inspection scheduling, record-number and permit-number handling, and compliance conditions relevant to facilities work-order progression.
used_for: blocking unsupported completion before inspection posture is clear

authoritative_sources.title: UFGS 01 33 00.05 20 - Construction Submittal Procedures
authoritative_sources.publisher: Whole Building Design Guide / USACE / NAVFAC / AFCEC
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Unified Facilities Guide Specifications are recognized federal facilities workflow standards. This guide defines preconstruction submittals, proposed subcontractor lists, schedules, health and safety plans, test reports, O&M data, and closeout submittals that govern whether field work is ready to progress.
used_for: evidence handling for orderly progression and rework

authoritative_sources.title: UFGS 01 78 00 - Closeout Submittals
authoritative_sources.publisher: Whole Building Design Guide / USACE / NAVFAC / AFCEC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This recognized federal guide specification is a public baseline for revised project documents, warranty management, testing and balancing, O&M manuals, and cleanup records needed before facilities work can be represented as complete or ready for release.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Completion, closeout, and return-to-service evidence review

workflow_stages.name: Accepted work-order confirmation and authority check
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the accepted work package has the governing work order, asset, facility, permit, inspection, outage, trade, subcontractor, and tenant authority context before changing execution state.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Site-readiness and active-work validation
workflow_stages.description: Check site logs, access conditions, utility isolation, approved plans, permit status, safety observations, inspection posture, and subcontractor readiness before allowing work to advance.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and rework handling
workflow_stages.description: Use CMMS records, service reports, change documentation, deficiency evidence, and missing-artifact lists to decide whether work must hold, return for rework, or escalate.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Completion and return-to-service evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Require inspection results, test and balancing data, photos, O&M data, warranty records, and closeout package artifacts before completion is recorded or return to service is represented as safe.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Authority escalation and state reversal
workflow_stages.description: Stop or reverse progression when safety, inspection, permit, trade, design, or policy conflicts materially change whether work can continue, complete, or return to service.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

artifacts: work orders, service requests, and PM or corrective maintenance tickets
permits, approved plans, and jurisdiction instructions
inspection records, special inspection letters, and deficiency logs
site logs, utility isolation references, lockout-tagout records, and safety observations
schedule windows, outage notices, shutdown approvals, and subcontractor commitments
change orders, RFIs, and rework documentation
photos, balancing reports, test data, and installation evidence
O&M manuals, warranty records, closeout packages, and return-to-service evidence
systems_of_record: inspection and special inspection tracking system
asset inventory or BAS-linked facility record system

allowed: Assess whether documented facilities records support advancing, holding, reworking, or completing accepted work-order execution.
Prepare evidence-backed execution updates, completion records, exception summaries, field-status notes, and escalation packages.
Request missing prerequisites and traceability records before changing lifecycle state or representing return-to-service posture.
escalate: Permit status, inspection requirements, site safety conditions, outage controls, or closeout evidence materially change the answer.
Change-order, design, subcontractor, or tenant-policy conflicts affect whether work can advance or close.
Licensed trade, superintendent, permitting, inspection, or safety ownership is needed to resolve the issue.
refuse: Permit approval, code interpretation, or regulator-facing commitments
Licensed trade signoff, engineer-of-record judgment, or design approval
Completion, closure, or return-to-service claims without sufficient evidence

domain_failure_modes: Advancing facilities work based on generic ticket status labels instead of actual permit, site-readiness, inspection, outage, and subcontractor records.
Marking work complete while final inspection, balancing data, warranty records, O&M data, or closeout evidence is still missing.
Failing to reopen or reverse lifecycle state when new safety observations or failed inspections change the answer.
Allowing unresolved change-order, outage, or scope mismatch conditions to disappear from the exception queue.
Overreaching into code interpretation, licensed trade judgment, design authority, or financial approval because the task sits in a work-order queue.
Representing provisional field completion as authorized return to service without explicit inspection and release evidence.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific permit, inspection, and restart requirements
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Permit classes, inspection checkpoints, and return-to-service evidence vary materially by authority having jurisdiction and facility system type.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require runtime retrieval of the governing jurisdiction, permit type, inspection checklist, and restart criteria before final progression or completion decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific authority thresholds for outage approval, rework approval, and return-to-service release
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public baselines define blocker handling, but tenant governance decides which facilities actions require facilities leadership, project controls, safety, or executive approval.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Retrieve the tenant authority matrix and keep outputs recommendation-only when approval ownership is unresolved.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because remaining ambiguity is explicitly constrained to tenant or jurisdiction retrieval at runtime.
The research output constrains the lane to facilities execution-state management with explicit completion-evidence discipline and permit, inspection, safety, outage, and licensed-trade escalation.
